name: Resonant leptogenesis
summary: Lowers the leptogenesis scale from the Davidson-Ibarra bound (M_R > 10^9 GeV) down to the TeV regime by exploiting CP-asymmetry resonant enhancement when two heavy Majorana neutrinos are nearly degenerate (mass splitting ~ width). The resonance amplifies the CP asymmetry by orders of magnitude, allowing TeV-scale leptogenesis testable at colliders.

addresses_puzzles.note: Reproduces eta ~ 6e-10 with M_R ~ TeV when two Majorana neutrinos are tuned to mass-degeneracy at the level of their decay width. The fine-tuning is what's costed in the compression score; the mechanism otherwise inherits seesaw structure.
Builds on type-I seesaw with TeV-scale heavy Majorana neutrinos.

touches_benchmarks.note: TeV-scale heavy neutrinos generate mu -> e gamma at observable rates if mixings are sizable; current MEG bound BR < 4.2e-13 constrains parameter space.
TeV heavy Majorana neutrinos contribute to 0nubb via short-range exchange; KamLAND-Zen sensitive.

introduces.note: Builds on seesaw_type_i. New parameters: degeneracy splitting, CP phase, M_R scale.

history: Pilaftsis (1997), 'CP violation and baryogenesis due to heavy Majorana neutrinos', Phys. Rev. D 56, 5431, arXiv:hep-ph/9707235. Pilaftsis-Underwood (2003), 'Resonant leptogenesis', arXiv:hep-ph/0309342, refined the formalism. Active research target as a TeV-scale leptogenesis alternative to high-scale.

references.citation: Pilaftsis, 'CP violation and baryogenesis due to heavy Majorana neutrinos', Phys. Rev. D 56, 5431 (1997)
Pilaftsis and Underwood, 'Resonant leptogenesis', Nucl. Phys. B 692, 303 (2004)
